title: Parsanol Ruby 1.2.2: FFI Captures, Scopes, and Dynamic Atoms
summary: Ruby bindings now support native capture extraction, isolated scopes, and context-sensitive parsing via FFI callbacks.

content.text: Parsanol Ruby 1.2.2 brings full FFI support for the new features introduced in parsanol-rs 0.3.0. Ruby code can now use capture atoms, scope atoms, and dynamic atoms with native performance.

require 'parsanol'

parser = str('hello').capture(:greeting)
result = parser.parse('hello world')
puts result[:greeting].to_s  # => "hello"

# Type-driven parsing with FFI callbacks
parser = match('[a-z]+').capture(:type) >> str(':') >>
        dynamic { |ctx|
          case ctx[:type].to_s
          when 'int' then match('\d+')
          when 'str' then match('[a-z]+')
          else match('[a-z]+')
          end
        }

result = parser.parse('int:42')
puts result[:type].to_s  # => "int"
The StreamingBuilder module provides maximum performance by eliminating intermediate AST construction. Include `Parsanol::BuilderCallbacks` in your class to receive parse events directly.

items: FFI bindings for capture, scope, and dynamic atoms
Fixed native extension loading for precompiled gems

title: Parsanol 0.3.0: Captures, Scopes, and Dynamic Atoms
summary: Major release adding capture extraction, isolated scopes, and context-sensitive parsing.

content.text: Parsanol 0.3.0 introduces three powerful new features for extracting and managing parsed data: **Capture Atoms**, **Scope Atoms**, and **Dynamic Atoms**.
Extract named values from your input, like named groups in regular expressions but integrated into the parsing grammar.
Create isolated capture contexts for nested parsing. Captures made inside a scope are discarded when the scope exits.
Runtime-determined parsing via callbacks. Enable context-sensitive parsing.

items: Streaming parser capture persistence across chunks

title: Parsanol Ruby 1.1.0: Slice Support and Performance
summary: Added Slice objects for lazy extraction and streaming parser support for large files.

content.text: Parsanol Ruby 1.1.0 adds Slice objects for lazy extraction and streaming parser support for large files.
Extract values lazily without loading entire parse results into memory:

require 'parsanol'

parser = str('hello').repeat(1)
result = parser.parse('hellohellohello')

# Access results as Slice objects
result.each_slice do |slice|
  puts slice.to_s  # => "hello"
end
Parse large files without loading them entirely into memory:

# Stream parsing for large files
parser = Parsanol::StreamingParser.new(grammar)
parser.parse_file('large_data.json') do |result|
  process(result)
end

rows: Return Slice objects for lazy extraction

items: Streaming parser support for large files

title: Parsanol 0.2.0: Backend Abstraction and Bytecode VM
summary: Introduced backend abstraction with trait-based selection, Bytecode VM for faster parsing, and pre-commit hooks.

content.text: Parsanol 0.2.0 introduces backend abstraction with trait-based selection, a new Bytecode VM backend for improved performance, and pre-commit hooks for local development.
Choose the right parsing backend for your grammar with trait-based selection.
The new Bytecode VM backend compiles grammar to bytecode instructions for faster execution on linear patterns.

# Install pre-commit hooks
pre-commit install

# Or use the shell script directly
./.githooks/pre-commit
Checks: format, clippy, docs, machete, typos, semver.

items: **Bytecode VM**: VM-based parser for linear patterns
Pre-commit hooks for local CI checks

summary: Improved release infrastructure with automated gem builds and cross-platform support.

content.text: Parsanol Ruby 1.0.2 focuses on release infrastructure improvements for reliable gem distribution.
Precompiled native gems for major platforms:

Precompiled native gems for Linux, macOS, Windows
Automated release workflow via GitHub Actions
Fixed native extension loading for various platforms
Improved error messages for missing native extension

title: Parsanol Ruby 1.0.0: Initial Release
summary: First release of Parsanol Ruby bindings with native extension support and Parslet-compatible API.

content.text: Parsanol Ruby 1.0.0 provides Ruby bindings for the Parsanol parser combinator library with native extension support for maximum performance.
Familiar API for users coming from Parslet:
The native extension provides significant performance improvements over pure Ruby parsers:

items: Native extension for Rust FFI parsing

title: Parsanol 0.1.0: Initial Release
summary: First public release of Parsanol, a modern parser combinator library for Rust and Ruby.

content.text: Parsanol is a modern parser combinator library that combines the elegance of PEG parsing with the performance of Rust. It provides multiple parsing backends, rich error reporting, and seamless Ruby bindings.
Five integration approaches from pure Ruby to ZeroCopy+Slice for maximum flexibility and performance. Parslet-compatible API makes migration straightforward.

rows: Return Slice objects for lazy extraction

items: **Data formats**: JSON, CSV, XML, TOML, YAML
